landing.heroBefore: Moving from the Balkans, India, Turkey, Ukraine, or beyond to
landing.heroDescription: Answer five practical questions and Ria prepares a personalized PDF checklist for your likely residence route, documents, timeline, and next steps.
landing.problemTitle: Residence paperwork is confusing when nobody explains it.
landing.problemIntro: Riadence gives you a structured first step: build a Slovakia-specific checklist, keep the result as a PDF, and know when a complex case needs licensed help.

problems.title: Unclear document lists
problems.description: Different sources say different things, so it is hard to know what actually applies to your case.

problems.title: Agency costs too early
problems.description: Many people pay before they even understand the likely route or what is missing.

problems.title: Translation and apostille risks
problems.description: A document can look ready until an office asks for a translation, legalization, or newer version.

problems.title: Fear of one mistake
problems.description: Applicants often worry that one missing proof or wrong date can delay the whole process.

problems.title: Wrong route confusion
problems.description: Employment, study, family, and business routes can look similar until the paperwork starts.

problems.title: Appointments feel high stakes
problems.description: It is stressful to show up without knowing which questions or missing documents may come up.

landing.clarityEyebrow: From confusion to a clearer plan
landing.clarityTitle: What a structured checklist helps you avoid
landing.clarityDescription: Your answers become a clearer route, document plan, PDF timeline, and handoff summary.
avoidItems: Paying an agency before you understand your case
landing.pricingTitle: Riadence is free. Professional help is case by case.
landing.pricingDescription: There is no monthly subscription. Standard checklist generation stays free; complex legal work is priced directly by the licensed agency or lawyer handling the case.

pricingCards.title: Riadence checklist

pricingCards.description: Optional paid support for asylum, deportation defense, appeals, prior refusals, or unusually complex family reunification cases.

landing.workspaceDescription: Structured intake, multilingual instructions, and case handoff tools for professionals.
landing.finalDescription: Answer five questions, download your personalized PDF, then ask Ria any follow-up questions.

footer.description: Ria is your friendly, practical guide for organizing a Slovakia residence checklist. I am not a lawyer. This is general information, not legal advice. For complex cases, consult a licensed immigration professional.

howItWorks.title: Five questions. One practical Slovakia checklist.
howItWorks.description: The wizard does the structured work first. Ria chat is available afterwards when you need clarification.

steps.title: Answer 5 questions
steps.text: Tell Ria where you are from, why you are moving to Slovakia, and what you already have.

steps.title: Ria prepares your route
steps.text: Ria organizes your answers into a likely residence route, document gaps, and practical next steps.

steps.title: Download your PDF checklist
steps.text: Keep a personalized checklist with your route, document plan, timeline, official links, and disclaimer.

steps.title: Get email follow-up
steps.text: Receive your checklist reminder and return to Ria with follow-up questions after the wizard.

checklistFlow.noteText: Start with a structured route and document plan. Ria is not a lawyer, does not file applications, and cannot guarantee approval.

agency.title: Ria does not replace experts. She prepares better clients.
agency.description: Riadence stays free for standard checklist preparation. Licensed agencies and lawyers are most useful for genuinely complex cases: asylum, deportation defense, appeals, previous refusals, or family reunification with a difficult case history.
agency.summaryText: Applicant has a previous refusal and a family reunification route with disputed supporting evidence. Ria has organized the timeline and missing records for licensed professional review.

faq.title: Guidance first. Expert support when needed.
faq.description: Slovakia-focused answers for applicants from the Balkans, India, Turkey, Ukraine, Georgia, Vietnam, and other non-EU countries.

items.question: I'm from Serbia — do I need a work permit for Slovakia?
items.answer: Visa-free entry for a short visit is not the same as permission to work or live in Slovakia. For employment, Serbian citizens generally need the correct employment and residence authorization for their situation. Verify current requirements with Slovak authorities or a licensed immigration lawyer.

items.question: How is this different from asking ChatGPT?
items.answer: Riadence starts with a structured Slovakia-focused wizard instead of an open-ended chat. It organizes your inputs into a route and document plan and gives you a personalized PDF checklist.

items.question: What does the free PDF checklist include?
items.answer: It includes your likely route, documents you have, likely missing items, translation or apostille reminders, a timeline, official-source links, and questions to verify before an appointment.

items.question: Is Riadence a law firm? Can you guarantee my visa approval?
items.answer: No. Riadence is not a law firm, Ria is not a lawyer, and nobody can guarantee a residence or visa outcome. A government authority makes the final decision.

items.question: When are the 2026 changes to Slovak residence law taking effect?
items.answer: There is no single date covering every 2026 change. Each amendment can have its own effective date and transitional rules. Check Slov-Lex and confirm with an official authority or licensed lawyer.

items.question: What happens after I download the checklist? Do you follow up by email?
items.answer: You receive a welcome follow-up at the email you provided. Ria remains available after the wizard for practical questions. Riadence does not submit the application for you.

items.question: Do you handle complex cases like asylum or deportation?
items.answer: Asylum, deportation defense, appeals, prior refusals, and other high-risk cases need a licensed professional. Riadence may recommend a partner agency or lawyer.
